title: Sous-Vide Fermented Dark Chocolate Stir-Fry
description: A show-off Argentinean dish featuring fermented dark chocolate served as a tender stir-fry. Pescatarian-friendly and suitable for vegans and vegetarians.

ingredients.name: Dark Chocolate
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality dark chocolate for best results.

instructions: Place the dark chocolate in a sous-vide bag and seal it. Cook at 62°C for 8 hours.
In a small bowl, mix rice vinegar, salt, soy sauce, lime juice, and fermentation culture together until well combined.
Drain the cooked dark chocolate thoroughly to remove any excess liquid.
Heat olive oil in a wok or large pan over medium-high heat. Add drained dark chocolate and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Pour in the vinegar mixture and continue stirring until the chocolate is fully melted and evenly coated, about another minute.
Remove from heat and serve immediately while still hot.
